verses.text: Saul consents to Stephen's death; persecution scatters the church. Scattered seed. The blow only spreads the gospel wider.
Saul consents to Stephen's death; persecution scatters the church. Scattered seed. The blow only spreads the gospel wider.
Saul consents to Stephen's death; persecution scatters the church. Scattered seed. The blow only spreads the gospel wider.
They that were scattered preach; Philip in Samaria does miracles, and there is 'great joy.' Gospel advances. Persecution carries the word to new places.

They that were scattered preach; Philip in Samaria does miracles, and there is 'great joy.' Gospel advances. Persecution carries the word to new places.
Simon the sorcerer, who bewitched the people, believes and is baptized. A doubtful convert. Even the magician is drawn to the gospel.

Peter and John come, pray, and the Samaritans receive the Holy Ghost. Apostolic confirmation. The new believers are sealed by the Spirit.

Simon offers money for the gift; 'thy money perish with thee'; thy heart is not right. Sin exposed. The Spirit's gift cannot be bought.
Simon offers money for the gift; 'thy money perish with thee'; thy heart is not right. Sin exposed. The Spirit's gift cannot be bought.

Simon offers money for the gift; 'thy money perish with thee'; thy heart is not right. Sin exposed. The Spirit's gift cannot be bought.
Repent of this wickedness and pray; Simon asks them to pray for him. A call to repent. Even gross sin meets the offer of mercy.
Repent of this wickedness and pray; Simon asks them to pray for him. A call to repent. Even gross sin meets the offer of mercy.

An angel sends Philip toward Gaza; he meets an Ethiopian eunuch reading Isaiah. Divine appointment. God leads His servant to a seeking soul.
An angel sends Philip toward Gaza; he meets an Ethiopian eunuch reading Isaiah. Divine appointment. God leads His servant to a seeking soul.

An angel sends Philip toward Gaza; he meets an Ethiopian eunuch reading Isaiah. Divine appointment. God leads His servant to a seeking soul.
The Spirit says, Join thyself; 'how can I, except some man should guide me?' Hungry to learn. The seeker welcomes a teacher.
The Spirit says, Join thyself; 'how can I, except some man should guide me?' Hungry to learn. The seeker welcomes a teacher.
The Spirit says, Join thyself; 'how can I, except some man should guide me?' Hungry to learn. The seeker welcomes a teacher.
He reads of the lamb led to slaughter; 'Philip... preached unto him Jesus.' Christ in Isaiah. The Scripture opens to reveal the Saviour.
He reads of the lamb led to slaughter; 'Philip... preached unto him Jesus.' Christ in Isaiah. The Scripture opens to reveal the Saviour.

He reads of the lamb led to slaughter; 'Philip... preached unto him Jesus.' Christ in Isaiah. The Scripture opens to reveal the Saviour.
See, here is water; 'if thou believest with all thine heart'; he is baptized. Ready faith. Belief is owned and sealed at once.
See, here is water; 'if thou believest with all thine heart'; he is baptized. Ready faith. Belief is owned and sealed at once.

The Spirit catches Philip away; the eunuch 'went on his way rejoicing.' Joyful convert. The gospel reaches even to Africa.
